title: FRI Dehradun Recruitment 2026 - Walkin for 11 Project Scientist, Research Associate and More Posts
shortInfo: Forest Research Institute Dehradun (FRI Dehradun) Project Scientist, Research Associate, Junior Project Fellow, Project Assistant Notification 2026 has been released on 12 May 2026 on the official website fri.res.in. A total of 11 vacancies have been announced through the FRI Dehradun Recruitment 2026. Candidates Max: 45 years who have Doctoral Degree, M.Tech/M.E/M.Sc, M.Sc in Agriculture/Botany/Biotechnology/Chemistry/Biochemistry, Bachelor's degree in Science are eligible to apply for these Project Scientist, Research Associate, Junior Project Fellow, Project Assistant vacancies. The walk-in

basic_details.job_title: FRI Dehradun Recruitment 2026 - Walkin for 11 Project Scientist, Research Associate and More Posts
basic_details.post_name: Project Scientist, Research Associate, Junior Project Fellow, Project Assistant
basic_details.short_information: Forest Research Institute Dehradun (FRI Dehradun) Project Scientist, Research Associate, Junior Project Fellow, Project Assistant Notification 2026 has been released on 12 May 2026 on the official website fri.res.in. A total of 11 vacancies have been announced through the FRI Dehradun Recruitment 2026. Candidates Max: 45 years who have Doctoral Degree, M.Tech/M.E/M.Sc, M.Sc in Agriculture/Botany/Biotechnology/Chemistry/Biochemistry, Bachelor's degree in Science are eligible to apply for these Project Scientist, Research Associate, Junior Project Fellow, Project Assistant vacancies. The walk-in

age_limit.age_details: Project Scientist-III: 45 years, Project Scientist-II: 40 years, Research Associate & Project Associate: 35 years, Junior Project Fellow: 28 years (Relaxable by 5 years for SC/ST, Women, PH, OBC)
age_limit.details: Project Scientist-III: Maximum age 45 years. Project Scientist-II: Maximum age 40 years. Research Associate & Project Associate: Maximum age 35 years. Junior Project Fellow: Maximum age 28 years. Age relaxation is applicable by 5 years for SC/ST, Women, PH, and OBC candidates.

qualification.education_qualification: Doctoral Degree, M.Tech/M.E/M.Sc, M.Sc in Agriculture/Botany/Biotechnology/Chemistry/Biochemistry, Bachelor's degree in Science.
qualification.details: Project Scientist -III: Doctoral Degree in Forestry/ Botany from a recognized university or equivalent. Seven years' experience in research field. Research Associate -III: Doctoral Degree in GIS/ Geoinformatics or 3 years research / teaching experience after M.Tech./M.E./M.Sc. in GIS/ Geoinformatics from a recognized university or equivalent. Junior Project Fellow (JPF): First class M.Sc. in Forestry/Botany. Project Scientist -II: Ph.D. in Forestry(Forest Genetics /Tree Improvement / Forest Biotechnology). Minimum three years research experience in R&D of forest genetics / tree improvement/forest biotechnology. Project Associate: M.Sc. in Agriculture (Genetics and Plant Breeding) OR M.Sc. In Forestry (Forest Genetics / Tree Improvement) Project Associate: M.Sc. in Botany / Biotechnology. Project Associate: M.Sc. in Chemistry / Biochemistry Project Assistant (PA): Bachelor's degree in Science (Zoology, Botany, Chemistry).

faq.question: Q1. What is the last date to apply for the FRI Dehradun recruitment?
faq.answer: The recruitment is through a walk-in interview. Candidates need to attend the interview on the specified dates: 26th May 2026, 28th May 2026, and 29th May 2026, between 09:00 AM and 10:15 AM for registration.

faq.question: Q2. What are the different posts available in FRI Dehradun recruitment?
faq.answer: The available posts are Project Scientist -III, Research Associate -III, Junior Project Fellow (JPF), Project Scientist -II, Project Associate, and Project Assistant (PA).

faq.question: Q3. What is the educational qualification required for Project Scientist -III?
faq.answer: For Project Scientist -III, a Doctoral Degree in Forestry/ Botany from a recognized university or equivalent is required, along with seven years of experience in a research field.

faq.question: Q4. What is the age limit for Junior Project Fellow?
faq.answer: The maximum age limit for Junior Project Fellow is 28 years. Age relaxation is applicable for SC/ST, Women, PH, and OBC candidates.

faq.question: Q5. What is the salary for Project Associate posts?
faq.answer: The salary for Project Associate posts is Rs. 25,000/- (Twenty Five Thousand) only (fixed) per month.

faq.question: Q6. Where will the walk-in interview be conducted?
faq.answer: The walk-in interview will be conducted at the Forest Research Institute, Dehradun, P.O. New Forest, Dehradun, Uttarakhand, 248006.

faq.question: Q7. What documents should candidates carry for the walk-in interview?
faq.answer: Candidates must bring a copy of their bio-data, recent passport size photograph, and self-attested copies of all educational certificates and relevant documents.

faq.question: Q8. Is there any application fee for this recruitment?
faq.answer: No application fee is mentioned in the official notification for this recruitment.

faq.question: Q9. What is the job type for these positions?
faq.answer: The job type for all these positions is temporary.

faq.question: Q10. What is the registration time for the walk-in interview?
faq.answer: The registration time for the walk-in interview is between 09:00 AM to 10:15 AM on the respective interview dates.
